title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Behavior Case: Diagnostic Model
summary: The command prints unordered warnings without locations, hides skipped pages, reports only total duration, logs author content and local paths, and cannot compare a slow build with a prior release. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the diagnostic model increment in bounded actionable build and publication evidence report. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-diagnostic-model — Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_diagnostics_observability_5(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-diagnostic-model-1of936
# Competency: ssg-diagnostic-model.
# Case 6705: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_diagnostic_model_1of936(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1of936 = "1of936-639fo1-6705"
    assert evidence_variant_1of936
    evidence_axes_1of936 = (
        "reader1of936",
        "source6705",
        "parse639fo1",
        "route1of9366705",
        "artifact67051of936",
        "recovery639fo16705",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1of936)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Diagnostic Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 4462 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Diagnostic Model model · Theory Diagnostic Model.
steps.why: Retrieving Diagnostic Model through the concrete bounded actionable build and publication evidence report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Diagnostic Model model · Theory Diagnostic Model, keep Diagnostic Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Diagnostic Model model · Theory Diagnostic Model, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Diagnostic Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Diagnostic Model model · Theory Diagnostic Model, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 4462 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Diagnostic Model model · Theory Diagnostic Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A stack trace is the most actionable build diagnostic for authors.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-diagnostic-model-1of936.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-diagnostic-model-1of936(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_diagnostic_model_1of936\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?diagnostics < 0 or located < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?located != diagnostics)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unredacted_secrets > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using represent and severity plus specific case evidence.
